description_long: This object is a pan flute, consisting of a series of cylindrical tubes of varying lengths arranged in a straight line and bound together by two horizontal bands. The tubes are made of a wood-like material, each one slightly longer than the next, creating a stepped appearance from one end to the other. The binding bands are a deep purple color and appear to be made of a different material, possibly fabric or leather, tightly securing the tubes in place. The open ends of the tubes are visible, indicating that each tube is hollow and capable of producing a different pitch when blown across the top. The overall craftsmanship is simple yet functional, with no visible decorative engravings or embellishments. This type of instrument is commonly used in street performances, parks, or cultural events in urban settings.
description: A wooden pan flute with multiple tubes of varying lengths, bound together by purple bands. The tubes are arranged in a straight line, each producing a different note.
description_view_0: The pan flute is seen from an angled perspective, clearly showing the stepped arrangement of the tubes and the purple binding bands.
description_view_1: Viewed from the end, only the circular openings of the tubes are visible, forming a compact line.
description_view_2: A direct side view reveals the full length of the tubes, highlighting the gradual increase in tube length from left to right.
description_view_3: From the opposite end, the circular openings of the tubes are again visible, with the longest tube in the foreground.
